data.title: Singtel and Qualcomm complete POC for AI-enabled home wifi gateway
data.description:
The telco plans to commercialise the gateway.

Singtel and Qualcomm Technologies completed a Proof of Concept (POC) for an AI-enabled Home WiFi Gateway, which identifies and prioritises network traffic based on application type.

The POC tested several applications across various devices to determine the improved experience through the AI-powered Smart Traffic Classifier.

The Smart Traffic Classifier can deliver up to 50% average latency reduction for applications such as gaming, streaming, and voice and video calls in congested environments.

After the POC, Singtel will commercialise the gateway in Singapore and expand it across markets within the Singtel group.

data.title: DBS and SMF partner to drive sustainability in SG’s manufacturers
data.description:
They aim to support 500 small and mid-sized manufacturers.

DBS and the Singapore Manufacturing Federation (SMF) have signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) to accelerate the adoption of sustainable practices within the local manufacturing sector.

The collaboration aims to support 500 small and mid-sized manufacturers over the next two years.

It will offer three services: training and upskilling with webinars and in-person workshops; sustainability programmes for a tailored transition, which include an ESG Ready Programme and an Officer-as-a-Service Programme; and sustainable loan options.

The partnership will combine DBS’ expertise in financial solutions and sustainability advisory with SMF’s industry knowledge, to provide manufacturers with practical support at every stage of their decarbonisation journey.

This is SMF’s first partnership with a financial institution, according to a statement by DBS and the SMF.

Manufacturing contributes approximately a fifth (20%) of SIngapore’s GDP. But it is also one of the nation’s largest carbon emitters, responsible for nearly half of the island’s greenhouse gas emissions, DBS and SMF said, citing data from the National Climate Change Secretariat (NCCS).

data.title: Thakral Corporation more than triples net profit to $28.8m in FY2024
data.description:
Its revenue rose by 36% YoY to $288.8m.

Thakral Corporation more than tripled its net profit attributable to shareholders to $28.8m in FY2024, up from $8.1m in the previous year.

The group’s revenue rose by 36% year-on-year (YoY) to $288.8m. Revenue from the lifestyle business grew by 37.4% to $273m; whilst revenue from its investment segment increased by 16.3% to $15.7m.

Moreover, the share of profit from associates decreased to $22.5m, proportionate to its reduced stake in the group’s associate in Australia, GemLife, to 31.7% as part of the TCH restructuring, as well as the absence of the one-off profit realised in the previous financial year from the disposal of a hotel property in Osaka, Japan.

GemLife achieved settlement of 355 homes in FY2024. As of 31 December 2024, total occupied homes stood at 1,804 across 10 resorts, approximately 24% more than a year ago.

data.title: Hotel Properties’ profit plummets 95% in FY2024
data.description:
The company said it was due to higher finance costs and fair value loss.

Hotel Properties’ net profit attributable to shareholders sank 95% year-on-year to $27.2m during the financial year 2024, down from $561m in the previous year.

The company said it was due to higher finance costs and fair value loss of the company’s London properties.

Revenue was 7.9% higher year-on-year to $692.9m driven by the opening of Six Senses Kanuhura in late 2023 and Four Seasons Hotel Osaka in August 2024.

Moreover, the group generated an operating profit of $180.4m, 23% higher than the previous year.

data.title: IHH Healthcare’s profit declines 10% to $815.9m in FY 2024
data.description:
Revenue stood at $7b (RM24.4b), up 16% YoY.

IHH Healthcare’s profit after tax and minority interests declined by 10% year-on-year (YoY) to $815.9m (RM2.7b) for the financial year ended 31 December 2024 (FY 2024).

FY 2024 revenue stood at $7b (RM24.4b), up 16% YoY.

Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortisation increased by 17% to $1.6b (RM5.4b).

RM1 = $0.30

data.title: APAC Realty’s profit after tax declines 38.5% in FY2024
data.description:
This is despite a slightly higher revenue.

The profit after tax of APAC Realty Limited fell 38.5% year-on-year to $6.5m in the financial year 2024 despite a slightly higher revenue. 

The company recorded a $561m revenue during the year, inching up by 0.7%. 

However, its net profit was dragged by lower contributions from new home sales, which typically yield higher margins.

data.title: Tuan Sing net profit sinks 52% in FY2024
data.description:
The group’s revenue decreased by 37% YoY.

Tuan Sing Holdings Limited’s net profit attributable to shareholders dropped by 52% year-on-year (YoY) to $2.3m for the financial year 2024, driven by a decline in occupancy rate.

The group’s revenue decreased by 37% YoY to $192.5m due to lower contributions from Real Estate Development.

Revenue from the segment fell by 70% YoY to $44.9m due to lower progressive revenue recognition of units sold in Peak Residence.

Moreover, revenue from Real Estate Investment also sank 11% YoY to $53.4m due to lower contributions from Link@896 in Singapore.

The Hospitality segment remained stable with a revenue of $88.4m.

The company declared an earnings per share of $0.19.

data.title: Mermaid Maritime’s earnings up nearly 44% in FY 2024
data.description:
Revenues surged by 89.2% during the period.

Mermaid Maritime Public Company Limited has reported a profit attributable to owners of the parent of $18.62m (US$13.78m) in fiscal year (FY 2024), up 43.6% from the previous year.

According to its disclosure, Mermaid Maritime said its revenue jumped 89.2% to $709.82m (US$525.27m) last year.

A major contributor was the increase in revenue from the rendering of services, which added $693.7m (US$513.34m), up 86.4% from last year. This was attributed to growth in all service sectors which were subsea inspection, repair and maintenance services, cable laying services, and subsea transportation and installation and decommissioning services.

Also fuelling revenue was the surge in the reversal of impairment losses on property, plant, and equipment to $13.39m (US$9.91m).

“The impairment losses on two owned Subsea vessels were reversed as the recoverable amounts of these vessels, determined as the higher of fair value less costs of disposal and value in use, exceeded their carrying amounts impaired in prior periods,” Mermaid Maritime said.

Expenses also increased last year, jumping 88.9% to $689.82m (US$510.47m).

The company said it remains cautiously optimistic about the global economy and the subsea industry. Amidst macroeconomic and geopolitical uncertainties it “remains well-positioned to navigate these complexities,” thanks to its strategic market presence, operational resilience, and strong financial discipline.

Mermaid Maritime said it will also continue enhancing its capabilities in offshore and subsea services whilst actively monitoring developments in the clean energy space. 

$1 = US$0.74

data.title: Jardine Cycle & Carriage profit falls 22% in 2024
data.description:
Revenue remained unchanged at $30.1b ($22.3b).

Jardine Cycle & Carriage (JC&C) reported a 22% decline in profit attributable to shareholders, falling to $1.28 b ($946m) in 2024.

The company’s full-year revenue remained unchanged at $30.1b ($22.3b).

Underlying profit fell 5% to $1.48b ($1.1b), mainly due to foreign exchange differences. Without currency translation impacts, it would have risen 3%.

Corporate costs rose from $70.1m ($52m) to $111.9m ($83m), following a swing from a $29.7m ($22m) foreign exchange gain in 2023 to a $22.9m ($17m) loss in 2024. Lower corporate net debt helped offset some of the increase, reducing financing charges by $10.8m ($8m).

JC&C cut its consolidated net debt—excluding Astra’s financial services subsidiaries—to $316.9m ($235m), down from $1.55b ($1.15b) in 2023. The Group’s corporate net debt dropped to $1.1b ($816m ), whilst Astra’s financial services net debt rose from $4.6b ($3.4b) to $5b ($3.7b).

data.title: Olam Group profit plunges 83.4% in H2 2024
data.description:
For the full year, it declined 69% YoY.

Olam Group reported an 83.4% year-on-year (YoY) decline in profit after tax and minority interest (PATMI) to $38.3m in the second half of 2024.

Despite the drop in PATMI, the group's revenue rose 24.0% YoY to $29.2b.

For the full year, PATMI fell 69% YoY to $86.4m, whilst revenue grew 16.3% to $56.2b.

The board has proposed a final dividend of $.03 per share, bringing the total dividend for 2024 to $.06 cents, down from $.07 in 2023.

As part of its restructuring, Olam will sell a 44.58% stake in Olam Agri to Saudi Agricultural and Livestock Investment Company (SALIC) for US$1.78b, valuing Olam Agri at US$4.00b. It also plans to divest its remaining 19.99% stake to SALIC after three years at the closing valuation plus a 6% internal rate of return (IRR).

The group said it will focus on unlocking value in its remaining businesses, including an IPO for Olam Food Ingredients (ofi).
